Package com.bfo.json

Class AbstractReader

java.lang.Object
com.bfo.json.AbstractReader
Direct Known Subclasses:
CborReader, JsonReader, MsgpackReader, YamlReader
public abstract class AbstractReader extends Object

The base reader class for file formats that generate a stream of JsonStream.Event objects. For many common cases the Json.read(java.lang.CharSequence) and Json.readCbor(java.io.InputStream) methods can be called as a simpler alternative to referencing this class.

Formats are text-based (eg Json) or binary-based (eg Cbor or Msgpack). Typically an AbstractReader will read a file in one go from a single input, 

  AbstractReader reader = new JsonReader();
  JsonBuilder builder = new JsonBuilder();
  reader.setFinal();
  reader.setInput(inputstream);
  reader.write(builder);
  Json json = builder.build();
However it is also possible to use multiple inputs, which is useful when data arrives in chunks - for example, when reading from a Selector. 
  AbstractReader reader = new JsonReader();
  JsonBuilder builder = new JsonBuilder();
  reader.setPartial();
  while (data = nextChunk()) {        // wait for next chunk
    reader.setInput(bytebuffer);
    reader.write(builder);
    if (builder.build()) {
      Json json = builder.build();
      return json;
    }
  }
  // Next block is required only for Json, see below.
  if (data == null) { // no more data coming
    reader.setFinal();
    json = builder.build();
    return json;
  }

This approach works for Cbor, Msgpack, and Json where the top level object is a map, list or string. If the top level object is a number, boolean or null, the setFinal() method must be used to inform the reader that no more data is coming (eg the number 12345 may arrive in two blocks, "123" and "45").

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    close()
    Close the current input
    long
    getByteNumber()
    Return the byte number of the input, or 0 if the input is not tracking bytes.
    long
    getCharNumber()
    Return the character number of the input, or 0 if the input is not text-based
    long
    getColumnNumber()
    Return the column number of the input, or 0 if the input is not text-based or is not tracking line numbers
    long
    getLineNumber()
    Return the line number of the input, or 0 if the input is not text-based or is not tracking line numbers
    abstract boolean
    hasNext()
     
    abstract JsonStream.Event
    next()
     
    AbstractReader
    setCharset(Charset charset)
    When reading text-based formats (eg Json) from an InputStream, set the Charset to use for decoding.
    AbstractReader
    setCodingErrorAction(CodingErrorAction action)
    Set the action to perform when converting an invalid byte sequence to UTF-8.
    AbstractReader
    setDraining()
    Indicate that after the read completes, the reader should verify there is no more content following the input.
    AbstractReader
    setFinal()
    Indicate that the current input is the final input.
    AbstractReader
    setInput(InputStream in)
    Set an InputStream to use as the next source for this reader.
    AbstractReader
    setInput(CharSequence in)
    Set an Readable to use as the next source for this reader, which must be a text-based format The input cannot be changed while hasNext() returns true.
    AbstractReader
    setInput(Readable in)
    Set an Readable to use as the next source for this reader, which must be a text-based format The input cannot be changed while hasNext() returns true.
    AbstractReader
    setInput(ByteBuffer in)
    Set an ByteBuffer to use as the next source for this reader.
    AbstractReader
    setInput(ReadableByteChannel in)
    Set an ReadableByteChannel to use as the next source for this reader.
    AbstractReader
    setLineCounting()
    Require text-based formats (eg Json) to keep track of line and column numbers when reading.
    AbstractReader
    setNoLineCounting()
    Permit text-based formats (eg Json) to not keep track of line and column numbers when reading.
    AbstractReader
    setNonDraining()
    Indicate that the Reader should try not read any more content after object, because more content may follow.
    AbstractReader
    setPartial()
    Indicate that the input may be followed by more input when it runs out.

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractReader

      public AbstractReader()

  • Method Details

    • hasNext

      public abstract boolean hasNext() throws IOException
      Throws:
      IOException

    • next

      public abstract JsonStream.Event next() throws IOException
      Throws:
      IOException

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • setFinal

      public AbstractReader setFinal()
      Indicate that the current input is the final input. This can be set after the read has started, and is required for situations where the end of a file format is ambiguous. Currently that's only when reading Json where the top-level item is a number, true, false or null. AbstractReaders are final by default.
      Returns:
      this

    • setPartial

      public AbstractReader setPartial()
      Indicate that the input may be followed by more input when it runs out. Reading cannot be changed from final to partial after the read has started.
      Returns:
      this

    • setDraining

      public AbstractReader setDraining()
      Indicate that after the read completes, the reader should verify there is no more content following the input. When reading from a stream, this also allows the stream to be read in blocks.
      Returns:
      this

    • setNonDraining

      public AbstractReader setNonDraining()
      Indicate that the Reader should try not read any more content after object, because more content may follow. For block-based inputs (eg Readable and ReadableByteChannel or inputs where there is no concept of a current position (eg a CharSequence), this is ignored.

    • setCodingErrorAction

      public AbstractReader setCodingErrorAction(CodingErrorAction action)
      Set the action to perform when converting an invalid byte sequence to UTF-8. The default is CodingErrorAction.REPLACE

    • setLineCounting

      public AbstractReader setLineCounting()
      Require text-based formats (eg Json) to keep track of line and column numbers when reading.

    • setNoLineCounting

      public AbstractReader setNoLineCounting()
      Permit text-based formats (eg Json) to not keep track of line and column numbers when reading.

    • setCharset

      public AbstractReader setCharset(Charset charset)
      When reading text-based formats (eg Json) from an InputStream, set the Charset to use for decoding. The default is to sniff the charset.

    • getByteNumber

      public long getByteNumber()
      Return the byte number of the input, or 0 if the input is not tracking bytes. Text-based formats (eg Json) will typically track characters not bytes

    • getCharNumber

      public long getCharNumber()
      Return the character number of the input, or 0 if the input is not text-based

    • getLineNumber

      public long getLineNumber()
      Return the line number of the input, or 0 if the input is not text-based or is not tracking line numbers

    • getColumnNumber

      public long getColumnNumber()
      Return the column number of the input, or 0 if the input is not text-based or is not tracking line numbers

    • close

      public void close() throws IOException
      Close the current input
      Throws:
      IOException

    • setInput

      public AbstractReader setInput(InputStream in)
      Set an InputStream to use as the next source for this reader. The input cannot be changed while hasNext() returns true.
      Returns:
      this

    • setInput

      public AbstractReader setInput(ReadableByteChannel in)
      Set an ReadableByteChannel to use as the next source for this reader. The input cannot be changed while hasNext() returns true.
      Returns:
      this

    • setInput

      public AbstractReader setInput(ByteBuffer in)
      Set an ByteBuffer to use as the next source for this reader. The input cannot be changed while hasNext() returns true.
      Returns:
      this

    • setInput

      public AbstractReader setInput(Readable in)
      Set an Readable to use as the next source for this reader, which must be a text-based format The input cannot be changed while hasNext() returns true.
      Returns:
      this

    • setInput

      public AbstractReader setInput(CharSequence in)
      Set an Readable to use as the next source for this reader, which must be a text-based format The input cannot be changed while hasNext() returns true.
      Returns:
      this

    • write

      public boolean write(JsonStream out) throws IOException
      Throws:
      IOException
